items.title: Azam to sue Albert Tei over allegations of intimidation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki will proceed with legal action against businessman Albert Tei over allegations made against him. He said he had instructed his lawyers to serve a legal notice on Tei as soon as possible. Previously, Azam had said he was considering launching a legal suit against Tei for slander.

items.title: Watchdog group demands maximum penalty after 'street race' that killed five
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A crime watchdog has called for the authorities to pursue the maximum penalties available under the law against those responsible for a street racing incident that claimed five lives, including four members of a family. Malaysians Against Rape, Assault and Snatch Thief (Marah) founder Dave Avran described the crash as a preventable tragedy caused by reckless and unlawful behaviour on a public road. "These victims were ordinary people going about their lives.

items.title: Perlis must innovate to tackle economic challenges, says Ruler
items.summary: KANGAR: The Perlis state government must adopt innovative approaches tailored to local strengths to address increasingly complex economic challenges, says the Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail. Also present were the Raja Perempuan of Perlis, Tuanku Tengku Fauziah Tengku Abdul Rashid; the Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail; and the Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil. Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin also stressed the importance of increasing state revenue and exploring new, more sustainable sources of income.

items.title: Police record Azam Baki’s statement in two separate probes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Police have recorded a statement from Tan Sri Azam Baki in connection with two separate investigations involving allegations of defamation, abuse of power, and criminal intimidation. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the statement was recorded under Section 112 of the Criminal Procedure Code. “The second investigation involves allegations of abuse of power and criminal intimidation against a member of the public identified as Albert Tei,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Six charged in RM192,000 mule account syndicate cases
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Six people, including a woman, were charged at the Sessions Court here today with offences related to mule accounts linked to non-existent loan, investment and job syndicates involving losses totalling RM192,084. Muhammad Aiman Firdaus Sezaini, 21; Fadzil Abd Aziz, 54; Eric Yeremia Kong, 27; and Sarawut Chaorai, 34, pleaded guilty after the charges were read before Judge Mohd Zamir Suhaimee and Datuk Che Wan Zaidi Che Wan Ibrahim. Aiman, Fadzil and Sarawut were fined RM1,000 each, while Kong was fined RM450.

items.title: Another PKR lawmaker claims access blocked to constituency fund portal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — The Petaling Jaya parliamentary office has been unable to access the MyKhas system since May 29, 2026, its MP Lee Chean Chung said in a statement today, making him the second lawmaker allegedly barred from the portal that provides access to constituency development funds. Lee said he issued the public statement to seek clarification from Putrajaya, stressing that critical matters related to constituency allocations and Projek Mesra Rakyat (PMR) have been disrupted. Both Lee and Subang MP Wong Chen are known allies of former minister and PKR defector Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli.

items.title: Fatal Serian crash: Car overturns while overtaking tractor
items.summary: KUCHING: A failed attempt to overtake a tractor claimed the lives of a family of five, including an infant, after their car veered off the road, overturned and landed in a water-filled drain in Serian today. Sarawak Police Commissioner Datuk Mohamad Zainal Abdullah said preliminary investigations revealed that the accident occurred at about 8.10am along Jalan Engsengei in Kampung Lubok Ria, when the vehicle was travelling towards Serian. The driver was identified as Mohammad Bistari Aidit, 30, while the four passengers were Nur Farah Shahirah Narudin, 26; Ajibah Ahmat, 47; Nur Ain Nasrin Narudin, 16; and two-year-old Nur As-syifa Lathfya Narudin.

items.title: Taiwan says Japan, Philippines must respect its rights in maritime border talks
items.summary: TAIPEI: Taiwan has asked that Japan and the Philippines respect its rights and territory when they hold talks on their maritime border, Taipei's foreign ministry said on Wednesday. Taiwan's foreign ministry said its representative offices in Japan and the Philippines would seek confirmation that the negotiation process and outcomes of any future agreements "will not affect the sovereign rights Taiwan enjoys under international law." Taiwan's sovereignty over its territory and relevant waters, as well as its rights under international law, are "indisputable", the ministry said.

items.title: Perlis receives over RM660mil under 13th Malaysia Plan – Perlis Ruler
items.summary: KANGAR: Perlis has received RM661.70 million under Rolling Plan 1 (RP1) of the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP), with expenditure reaching RM87.46 million, or 13.22 per cent, as of April. The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, said the allocation covered 354 projects. "Under Rolling Plan 5 (RP5) of the 12th Malaysia Plan (12MP), Perlis received RM632.56 million involving 188 projects.

items.title: Teacher pleads not guilty to seven sexual assault charges involving two students in Terengganu
items.summary: SETIU, June 3 — A male teacher pleaded not guilty at the Setiu Sessions Court today to seven charges of physical sexual assault against two male students since last May. Darusalam Ab Rahman, 42, made the plea after all the charges were read out before Judge Ahmad Fadhli Mahmud. The charges were framed under Section 12(1) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act (SOACA) 2017 and read together with Section 16(1) of the same Act, which provides for a prison sentence not exceeding five years and liability to whipping.

items.title: Ukrainian haiku poet finds small miracles in war
items.summary: A temperamental lift leads to the apartment in central Ukraine of a 27-year-old poet celebrated in Japan but almost unknown in her own country. With pink hair, fuchsia sweater and matching socks, Vladislava Simonova tells the story of her burgeoning career 7,800 kilometres (4,850 miles) away in a country she has never visited. Simonova said she discovered haiku – her preferred form – in 2013, when she was a teenager.

items.title: 95pct of area searched; search for Jaslinda to be scaled down
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Search and rescue (SAR) personnel have covered about 95 per cent of the designated search area in their efforts to locate missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin at Gunung Batu Putih, Tapah. District police chief Superintendent Abdul Malik Hasim said the extensive search effort, involving 116 personnel from multiple agencies over the past 11 days, was among the factors behind the decision to scale down the operation beginning tomorrow. Abdul Malik said the decision was reached during a SAR coordination meeting chaired by the Batang Padang district officer earlier today.

items.title: Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin wants Perlis MB given space to govern until end of assembly term
items.summary: KANGAR, June 3 — The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, wants Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah to be given the space to continue leading the state administration until the end of the current Perlis State Assembly term without any interference. His Royal Highness said political stability is a crucial factor that will influence investors’ confidence to invest in the state. As such, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin called on all state assemblymen to set aside their egos and extend full and continuous cooperation to Abu Bakar for the benefit of the people and the state.

items.title: Malaysia's gas supply stable, no immediate shortage risk: MOSTI, Gas Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's gas supply remains stable with no immediate risk of shortages despite ongoing uncertainty in global energy markets. Gas Malaysia president and group chief executive officer Azli Mohamed said there is currently no shortage of gas supply, with volumes received from Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) sufficient to meet demand. While gas supply remains adequate, Azli said broader challenges facing industries relate more to transportation costs and supply chain disruptions.

items.title: Batu Kawan Industrial Park issues settled following PDC, Aspen meeting
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang Development Corporation (PDC) and Aspen (Group) Holdings Ltd have agreed to consider all matters relating to the Batu Kawan Industrial Park 2 (BKIP2) request for proposal (RFP) exercise formally closed. PDC said, as the evaluating authority, it had exercised its full discretion and prerogative in assessing all proposals submitted under the RFP exercise. Aspen acknowledged and respected PDC's authority as the evaluating body and said it did not challenge the outcome of the RFP exercise.

items.title: Perlis Ruler wants MB be given space to govern without interference
items.summary: KANGAR: The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, has called for Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah to be given space to lead the state administration until the end of the current state assembly term without interference. His Royal Highness said political stability was crucial in boosting investor confidence in the state. Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin said political differences should not become an obstacle to development and investment efforts in the state.

items.title: Azam Baki quizzed over Bloomberg article, Albert Tei claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police today recorded a statement from former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki to facilitate two probes. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the first investigation involved the publication of an article by Bloomberg.com which allegedly contained defamatory claims over ownership of Velocity Capital Partner Bhd shares. "The second case involves allegations of abuse of power and criminal intimidation against a member of the public named Albert Tei," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Lab assistant facing murder charge over Klang delivery rider’s death pleads not guilty to drug offence
items.summary: KLANG, June 3 — A laboratory assistant who was previously charged with killing a motorcyclist in a road crash changed his plea to not guilty on one drug-related charge in the Magistrate’s Court here today. R. Saktygaanapathy, 28, changed his plea after the amended charge was read out again before Magistrate Nor Azilah Mat. According to the amended charge, he allegedly administered tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) into his own body at the Narcotics Criminal Investigation Division Office, South Klang District Police Headquarters (IPD) at 3.30 pm on March 29, 2026.

items.title: Convenience store opened only five days before robbery
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL: The convenience store in Sungai Daun here, that was robbed by armed assailants, had been operating for only five days before the incident, neighbouring traders said. The traders, who spoke to the New Straits Times on condition of anonymity, said they had not had the chance to know the store operator personally yet. Only the convenience store operated at night.

items.title: Mikro MSC, HKCT to explore large-scale battery storage projects in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mikro MSC Bhd has entered into a memorandum of agreement with Hong Kong Cospower Technology Co Ltd (HKCT) to explore the development of large-scale utility Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) projects in Malaysia. The partnership is aligned with the country's energy transition agenda under the National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR), which is expected to unlock investment opportunities worth between RM1.2 trillion and RM1.3 trillion by 2050. Both Mikro MSC and HKCT will also focus on opportunities under the NETR through the Malaysia Battery Energy Storage System (MyBeST) programme, the country's first competitive open-bidding initiative for grid-scale energy storage projects regulated by the Energy Commission.

items.title: Police urge MHO to share data on alleged Malaysia scam hubs
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Police have urged the Malaysian Humanitarian Organisation (MHO) to share information regarding claims that online scam syndicates previously operating in Myanmar, Laos and Cambodia have relocated their operations to Malaysia. Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said the allegations could not be verified at this stage, but assured that police would look into any information provided. Hishamuddin said MHO would soon submit information on the alleged scam centres to the authorities.

items.title: Former Johor MB Hasni plays down parliamentary move as BN candidate speculation mounts
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Speculation has been swirling around former Johor menteri besar Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad and whether he will defend his Benut seat. However, when contacted by the New Straits Times , Hasni said discussions with Johor Barisan Nasional chairman and caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, had so far indicated no change to the existing arrangement. "As of my last discussion with Datuk (Onn Hafiz), the status quo remains," he said.

items.title: 'Papa is gone, I'd rather be dead': Son of delivery rider killed in crash haunted by guilt
items.summary: KLANG: The death of delivery rider Amirul Hafiz Omar in a collision at Jalan Raya Barat in March has plunged his family into darkness, particularly his three young children. His widow, Nor Nadia Abdul Majid, 32, said her eldest son, Muhammad Aqif Amsyar, nine, continues to struggle with guilt, believing he was responsible for his father's death. Amirul, 32, was on his way to buy a mathematics book for him when the fatal accident occurred.

items.title: Trooping of the Colours reflects armed forces' loyalty to King and nation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Trooping of the Colours ceremony reflects the armed forces' supreme dedication and unwavering loyalty to the Yang di-Pertuan Agong and the nation, while symbolising respect for military history and tradition. Commander of the 12th Malaysian Infantry Brigade, Brigadier-General Mahamad Fazli Mat Idris said the Colours represented sovereignty within the armed forces and are regarded as the highest symbols of honour, dignity and unit identity. He said the ceremony was among the most significant in the military calendar, traditionally held three times during the Yang di-Pertuan Agong's five-year reign – in the first, third and final years.

items.title: SAR for missing hiker Jaslinda continues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, at Gunung Batu Putih near Tapah, Perak, will continue, albeit on a smaller scale, police said today. Tapah district police chief Superintendent Abdul Malik Hasim said authorities remain committed to locating Jaslinda despite the operation entering its later stages on Day 11. He said the SAR operation currently involves 116 personnel from multiple agencies.

items.title: Johor PKR grassroots back Prakash for Tiram seat
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Calls are growing among Johor Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) grassroots members for Pakatan Harapan (PH) to field Tebrau PKR chief M. Prakash as its candidate for the Tiram seat in the upcoming state election. State grassroot supporters urged party leaders to field a candidate with strong community credentials. Supporters view Prakash as a long-serving grassroots leader with consistent engagement at community level.

items.title: Police tracking down suspects in viral Kaamatan brawl in Penampang
items.summary: The incident, which went viral on social media, is believed to have stemmed from a misunderstanding between several people. Penampang deputy police chief Deputy Superintendent Erwansyah Abdul Basir said preliminary findings showed no weapons were used in the fight, which was later broken up by members of the public. Earlier, several clips showing a group fighting outside the KDCA grounds went viral online and drew public attention.

items.title: Post-mortem report on lost hiker's death pending laboratory analysis
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: The post-mortem findings into the death of Nur Izzati Humaira Azizul, whose body was discovered after she went missing while hiking at Bukit Changkat Asa in Hulu Selangor last week, remain pending laboratory analysis. Hulu Selangor deputy police chief Deputy Superintendent Mohd Asri Mohd Yunus said police were still awaiting the full reports from the Chemistry Department and medical specialists at Sungai Buloh Hospital. Following the discovery of her body, police classified the case as a sudden death report pending further investigation.

items.title: Ex-bouncer gets five years' jail for blinding man in nightclub assault
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A former nightclub bouncer was jailed five years for assaulting a man and leaving him blind in his right eye at a nightclub 10 years ago. Sessions Court judge Mazuliana Abdul Rashid handed down the sentence against Jamsari Idris, 53, after he was found guilty to the offence at the end of defence case today. In her judgment, Mazuliana rejected the defence's argument that the accused had merely calmed the victim, ruling that his testimony was unreasonable.

items.title: Man arrested for raping 16-year-old he met just 3 days earlier
items.summary: District police chief Superintendent Amiruddien Sariman said police received a report on the incident at 1.51am on May 28. Acting on information received, a team from the Tampin police headquarters' Criminal Investigation Department arrested the suspect at his home in Kuala Pilah about 10.10pm yesterday. Amiruddien said investigations revealed that the victim and suspect had exchanged phone numbers and had been acquainted for only three days before the incident.

items.title: SRKK AI inks underwriting deal with TA Securities ahead of ACE Market IPO
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Digital transformation and technology solutions provider SRKK AI Bhd has signed an underwriting agreement with TA Securities Holdings Bhd ahead of its initial public offering (IPO) on the Ace Market of Bursa Malaysia. The IPO entails a public issue of 64 million new shares and an offer for sale of 13 million shares. SRKK AI implements end-to-end digital transformation solutions for enterprises and embeds artificial intelligence (AI) into enterprises' information technology (IT) lifecycles, across cybersecurity, business workflows, cloud, software and analytics.

items.title: Negri Sembilan risks constitutional crisis if assembly misses June 10 deadline
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Negri Sembilan could face a constitutional crisis by June 10 if the State Legislative Assembly does not convene before that date, according to a former Barisan Nasional (BN) strategist. Former BN strategic communications deputy director Datuk Eric See-To said the Negri Sembilan Constitution requires the state assembly to meet within six months of its previous sitting, which was held on Dec 11, 2025. "This means that if an actual state assembly sitting is not held by June 10 at the latest, Negri Sembilan could face a very serious constitutional issue," he said in a Facebook post.

items.title: Man jailed three months for outraging sister-in-law's modesty
items.summary: Magistrate Arun Noval Dass meted out the sentence after the 26-year-old married accused pleaded guilty to the charge. Based on the facts of the case, the victim lodged a police report stating that the accused entered her room, pulled her hands, and forced her to lie down. When the accused attacked her, the victim scolded him.

items.title: Ford to recall nearly 420,000 US vehicles over seat-belt issue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ford Motor will recall 419,967 vehicles in the US as seat belts that do not retract or extend properly can fail to restrain an occupant as intended, increasing the risk of injury in a crash, the US National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Wednesday. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: OECD cuts 2026 global growth forecasts over Mideast war fallout
items.summary: PARIS: The war in the Middle East has dented economic growth prospects worldwide, with a more severe shock likely if no effective ceasefire is agreed before 2027, the OECD warned Wednesday. Global economic growth is now forecast to reach 2.8 percent this year if Gulf exports of oil and gas return to pre-conflict levels in the third quarter, the group of 38 industrialised countries said in its quarterly update. If the Mideast war continues into next year, however, global growth could slow to 2.1 per cent, the OECD said.

items.title: Perlis ruler urges state to pursue high-impact investments, explore new revenue sources for sustainable growth
items.summary: KANGAR, June 3 — The Perlis state government must explore innovative approaches suited to local potential to address increasingly complex economic challenges, said the Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail. Also in attendance was the Raja Perempuan of Perlis, Tuanku Tengku Fauziah Tengku Abdul Rashid. The Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail, and the Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil, were also present.

items.title: GOF foils bid to smuggle exotic birds from Thailand, seizes parrots worth RM100,000
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 3 — Four exotic birds worth approximately RM100,000, believed to have been smuggled from Thailand, were seized by the General Operations Force (GOF) in an operation near the illegal Kak Nah jetty in Pengkalan Kubor, Tumpat, yesterday. GOF Southeast Brigade Commander SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the seizure was carried out under Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan at about 7 pm after a Strike Force team detected a man unloading several cages on the Malaysian side of Sungai Golok. “Inspection of the abandoned cages found two Blue-and-Gold Macaws and two Blue-fronted Amazon parrots, believed to have been brought in without valid permits.

items.title: Perlis receives RM661.7m under 13MP as ruler calls for faster project implementation
items.summary: KANGAR, June 3 — Perlis has received an allocation of RM661.70 million under the Rolling Plan 1 (RP1) of the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP), with expenditure performance reaching RM87.46 million, or 13.22 per cent, as of last April. The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, said the spending involved the implementation of 354 projects. “Under the Rolling Plan 5 (RP5) of the 12th Malaysia Plan (12MP), Perlis received an allocation of RM632.56 million involving 188 projects.

items.title: Chinese national pleads not guilty to murdering compatriot at Cheras shopping mall restaurant
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — A former restaurant waitress, a Chinese national, pleaded not guilty in the High Court here today to murdering her female fellow national by stabbing her multiple times at a restaurant in a shopping mall in Cheras two years ago. The woman was accused of murdering her colleague, cashier Zhang Wei, 34, at 4.30pm on November 29, 2024. Deputy public prosecutor (DPP) Faridah Nurdinie Bahrum appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was unrepresented.

items.title: Woman pleads guilty to culpable homicide over death of one of her newborn twins in Johor Bahru
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 3 — A 22-year-old woman pleaded guilty at the Sessions Court here today to a charge of culpable homicide over the death of one of her newborn twin daughters last month. According to the charge sheet, she was accused of causing the death of a baby girl with the intention of causing bodily injury likely to result in death. Under Section 304(a) of the Penal Code, bail may be granted at the discretion of the Sessions Court or High Court.

items.title: Man swept away in Sungai Pahang crossing found drowned
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 3 — A man believed to have been swept away by strong currents while attempting to cross Sungai Pahang at Kampung Peragap, Temerloh yesterday was found drowned today. A spokesman for the Pahang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) said the body of Samsul Azmi Ishury, 41, was discovered at 9.35am, approximately 2.2 kilometres from Kampung Loyang, Temerloh. Apart from the fire and rescue department, the operation also involved the police, the Malaysian Civil Defence Force (APM), and was assisted by local villagers.

items.title: Wet weather ahead: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms, heavy rain in five states until 3pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit five states until 3pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). It said that the areas involved are Perlis, Penang, and Kedah (Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Yan, Pendang and Kuala Muda). “Similar weather conditions are also expected in Sarawak, involving Sibu (Sibu and Selangau) and Mukah (Tanjung Manis and Daro),” MetMalaysia said in a statement.

items.title: Azam Baki grilled for two hours at Bukit Aman over threat allegations by businessman Albert Tei
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki was at Bukit Aman police headquarters today to give a statement over allegations by businessman Albert Tei that he had been threatened. Azam said he arrived at about 9am and gave his full cooperation to investigators, Sinar Harian reported today. Azam said he denied all allegations made by Tei.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan MB granted audience with Tuanku Muhriz at Istana Hinggap
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 3 — Yang Dipertuan Besar Negeri Sembilan Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir today granted an audience to Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun at Istana Hinggap here. Speaking to reporters afterwards, Aminuddin said the audience was part of his regular weekly schedule ahead of chairing the State Executive Council meeting. “It is the usual Wednesday meeting,” he said briefly.

items.title: Man suffers 50pc burns after suspected LPG explosion in Alor Gajah house
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, June 3 — A man suffered burns to about 50 per cent of his body after being hit by an explosion believed to have been caused by a liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) leak at a house in Taman Baiduri, Kuala Ina here today. Melaka Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) zone 2 chief, Assistant Fire Superintendent Zulkhairani Ramli said his department received an emergency call about the incident at about 10.26 am before a team of personnel from the Alor Gajah Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) was dispatched to the location. He said that while the victim was inspecting, a sudden explosion occurred which caused him to suffer burn injuries and serious damage to the house.

items.title: Retired civil servant loses nearly RM98,000 in TikTok investment scam
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 3 — A retired civil servant here lost RM97,933 after being duped by a fraudulent online investment syndicate. Kuala Terengganu district police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said that on May 14, the 59-year-old victim came across an investment advertisement on the TikTok application offering lucrative returns within a short period. The victim then contacted the suspect via Telegram and agreed to make an initial investment using his pension funds.

items.title: Fadhlina: Budget 2027 cost-saving measures will not affect key education initiatives
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Government cost-saving measures in drafting Budget 2027 will not affect the Ministry of Education’s (MOE) key plans, particularly in the recruitment of new teachers and the construction of additional classrooms, said Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek. In addition, Fadhlina said that repairs to dilapidated schools and building maintenance will continue as usual. Fadhlina said the MOE will also ensure that any cost-saving measures will not affect the rollout of the new curriculum next year, as well as the intake of six-year-old children into Year One.

items.title: Businessman pleads not guilty to dangerous driving charge over teen’s death in Kuala Terengganu crash
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 3 — A businessman pleaded not guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of dangerous driving that caused the death of a teenage boy in an accident at Jalan Losong Feri in May. Asrizal Hisham Khalid, 43, was charged with dangerous driving a Volkswagen GTI car that caused the death of Wah Han Keong, 18, at 3.36 pm, on May 19. In addition, the accused also faces a disqualification from holding or obtaining a driving licence for a period of not less than five years from the date of conviction.

items.title: Labuan MP urges landlords to vet tenants after 57 arrested in online scam raid
items.summary: LABUAN, June 3 — Labuan Member of Parliament Datuk Suhaili Abd Rahman has urged property owners on the duty-free island to exercise greater caution when renting out their premises to prevent them from being misused for online scam operations. He said the recent police raid at a commercial building in downtown Labuan, which led to the arrest of 57 individuals believed to be involved in an international investment scam syndicate, should serve as a lesson to all property owners. “The tenants had apparently misrepresented the purpose of renting the premises, claiming that the space would be used for agro-related investment activities.

items.title: Bersama to contest Johor polls, launches nationwide ‘Jelajah Kancil’ campaign tour
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) has confirmed it will contest the Johor state election in 2026, marking its first electoral outing since being revived on May 17. Alongside its electoral plans, Bersama will launch its nationwide campaign tour, Jelajah Kancil, beginning June 6 at the Danga Bay Convention Centre at 8pm. Stops include Melaka (June 7), Negeri Sembilan (June 13), Perak (June 14), Pahang and south Terengganu (June 21), north and central Selangor (June 28), south Selangor (July 1), Kelantan and north Terengganu (June 20), Pulau Pinang (July 4), Kedah and Perlis (July 5), Kuala Lumpur (Setiawangsa, July 11), Selangor Barat (Shah Alam, July 12) and Selangor Timur (Pandan, July 18).

items.title: Five, including baby girl, killed after family vehicle crashes into water pipe in Serian
items.summary: KUCHING, June 3 — Five people, including a two-year-old girl, were killed in a single-vehicle crash along Jalan Baki Lama-Ensengei in Serian this morning. According to the Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba), firefighters from the Serian fire station were dispatched to the scene after receiving an emergency call at 8.41am. The incident involved five victims understood to be from one family, comprising a man, three women aged between 16 and 47, and a two-year-old girl.

items.title: Tabung Haji: Concealing Zamzam water in bags may result in luggage being opened and delayed
items.summary: MAKKAH, June 3 — Carrying large quantities of Zamzam water as souvenirs may result in complications, with haj pilgrims’ luggage subject to detention or inspection by Saudi authorities. Haj Pilgrims Operations deputy director Mohd Ridzuan Yusoff said that despite annual reminders, some pilgrims still attempt to bring extra Zamzam water home by concealing it in their luggage. Mohd Ridzuan said a more concerning risk is that pilgrims’ personal belongings may be misplaced or damaged during the inspection process if their luggage has to be opened.

items.title: Fadillah: Strategic partnerships between govt, industry and financiers key to accelerating Malaysia’s energy transition
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, said the successful convening of the second edition of Energy Transition Conference 2026 (ETCon26) by Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) is commendable as a significant achievement, as it has evolved into a credible platform that brings together leading minds from across the world to exchange ideas and drive progress in the energy sector. “The conference also reflects Malaysia’s strong commitment to accelerating the energy transition, signalling a decisive shift from ambition to execution as the country advances its efforts towards a more sustainable energy future,” he said at the Energy Transition Conference 2026 (ETCon26) here today. The first ETCon was held in conjunction with the National Energy Transition Roadmap in 2023.

items.title: Six shoplots razed in late-night fire in Tanjong Karang, no casualties reported
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 3 — Six shops, including a jewellery shop, were destroyed in a fire on Jalan Besar in Tanjong Karang near here last night. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said they were alerted to the incident at about 11.27 pm yesterday and dispatched personnel to the scene. “The operations commander reported that the fire involved six shoplots, each measuring 40ft by 20ft.

items.title: PLUS urges motorists to plan return journey using MyPLUS-TTA ahead of peak congestion from June 4–7
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 3 — Plus Malaysia Berhad (PLUS) is urging motorists returning to the Klang Valley after the Hari Raya Aidiladha and extended festive holidays to plan their journeys early using the MyPLUS-TTA digital travel schedule to avoid heavy congestion. The highway concessionaire said it expects a significant surge in traffic volume across its network during the return commute period, particularly on Peak Days identified as June 4, 5, 6 and 7, 2026. As part of its festive traffic management plan, PLUS said Smart Lane routes have been activated at 33 strategic locations along its highways.

items.title: Police tracking suspect who attacked man with machete in Nibong Tebal armed robbery
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL, June 3 — Police are tracking down a male suspect who attacked a man with a machete during an armed robbery at a convenience store in Taman Bistari here last Sunday. South Seberang Perai district deputy police chief DSP Mohd Norazmi Abd Ghapar said the victim, 27, sustained injuries to his head and both arms during the robbery at 9.29 pm, during which the suspect escaped with cash, several boxes of cigarettes and a sling bag with an estimated loss of RM500. He also urged those with information about the robbery to contact investigating officer, Insp Noremifarahana Tuan Roslan at 04-5858222 or the nearest police station to assist investigations.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh: RM6.5m Lembah Kiara Park upgrade completed, most funds spent on drainage and infrastructure works
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Upgrading works at Lembah Kiara Park costing RM6.5 million have been completed, with most of the allocation spent on rehabilitating the park’s drainage system, retention ponds and electrical infrastructure. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the recreational park, located within Bukit Kiara Federal Park, is managed by the National Landscape Department (JLN) and not Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL). She said the remaining RM3.3 million was used for pond and drainage rehabilitation works, including the restoration of the upstream pond, sediment basin, streams and the park’s main retention pond.

items.title: Pahang police to record more statements in probe into student’s claim of being slapped by politician
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 3 — Pahang police will record the statements of several witnesses to complete investigations of a public university student who claimed he was slapped by a politician in Janda Baik, Bentong on May 20. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the student, 23, lodged a police report at 12.11 pm last Saturday (May 30) about the alleged slapping incident that occurred on May 20 during a university programme. “The police have taken the statements of both the suspect and the victim and will record statements from several other witnesses to complete the investigation,” he said.

items.title: Guan Eng: Banks should do CSR with one-year interest pause for MSMEs' existing loans; interest-free RM50k new loans
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Malaysia’s banking industry should “step up and do its Corporate Social Responsibility” (CSR) by urgently helping out local micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) with loan relief measures, DAP’s Bagan MP Lim Guan Eng said today. Noting that banks’ before-tax profits grew from RM48.3 billion in 2024 to RM54.8 billion in 2025, Lim said the banks’ 13.5 per cent or RM6.5 billion growth in before-tax profits could help “offset” the expected contraction in growth for Malaysia’s manufacturing industry. Lim said the government must urgently use fiscal and monetary measures to help Malaysia’s 1.1 million MSMEs reduce costs and generate businesses, to counter the negative effects of an expected economic slowdown.

items.title: Beyond bricks and mortar, Nga Kor Ming says arts and culture are key to thriving cities
items.summary: IPOH, June 3 — A city cannot thrive on physical infrastructure alone as arts, culture and human connection are equally important in creating communities with a sense of belonging and identity, says Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming. “A city cannot thrive on physical infrastructure alone. Without arts, culture and human connection, even the most modern city risks losing its soul.
